name: Credit Restoration of Nevada
description_short: Nevada-based credit repair firm that disputes errors and negative items on credit reports using electronic and USPS-based challenges to all three credit bureaus.
description_long:
Credit Restoration of Nevada (CRN Nevada) is a credit repair company founded by Harry Jacobs, who hosts The Credit Show on Las Vegas radio (101.5 FM K-DAWN and News Talk 840 KXNT) and has served as a credit expert on local television stations including Fox5, KTNV (ABC 13), and KSNV (NBC3). Jacobs is FICO-certified and has provided expert witness testimony on more than 200 credit-related legal cases, positioning the company as a locally established credit repair operation with media visibility.

CRN Nevada offers a three-step credit restoration process: (1) Analysis of all three credit bureaus to identify inaccurate, outdated, and negative information at no cost; (2) Dispute initiation using both electronic communication and USPS mail to ensure compliance with the 30-day bureau response requirement; and (3) Progress monitoring through a customer portal that tracks initial scores, challenged items, and removal results. The company targets removal of late payments, foreclosures, bankruptcies, collections, charge-offs, tax liens, and credit errors. They also mention debt settlement services (up to 26% debt reduction) and credit card interest rate reduction (47% lower rates claimed).

CRN Nevada differentiates itself through founder Harry Jacobs' media presence and expertise in Fair Debt Collection Practices and Fair Credit Reporting Act. The company emphasizes a dual-channel dispute process (electronic and USPS) specifically designed to hold credit bureaus accountable to the 30-day response requirement. Customer testimonials highlight results like score improvements from 650 to 720 in 90 days and successful mortgage qualification after credit restoration.

The company operates primarily as a credit repair firm but references debt settlement and interest rate negotiation services that fall outside traditional credit dispute work. Typical timelines are advertised as 90-120 days for completion. The website lacks detailed pricing information, specific FCRA compliance disclosures, and clear terms of service, which are standard in the credit repair industry.

In the broader ecosystem of credit repair services, consumers have multiple paths to improving their credit. Professional credit repair companies can dispute inaccurate items with all three bureaus, while credit monitoring services provide ongoing alerts about changes to your reports. For those building credit from scratch, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured approaches. Consumers dealing with overwhelming debt may benefit from debt consolidation loans to simplify payments, or credit counseling through nonprofit agencies for personalized budgeting guidance. Consumers who successfully repair their credit often find better rates on installment loans, secured credit cards, and other financial products.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Free credit report analysis with no conditions or commitment required
Dual-channel dispute process using both electronic and USPS communication to enforce 30-day bureau compliance
Founder Harry Jacobs has FICO certification and 200+ expert witness cases in credit damages
Customer portal for real-time tracking of challenged items and removal results throughout the process
Founder's 12+ year media presence as radio host (The Credit Show) and frequent TV credit expert appearances
Targets multiple negative item types: late payments, foreclosures, bankruptcies, collections, charge-offs, tax liens, and errors
Testimonials cite specific results (score improvement from 650 to 720 in 90 days, mortgage qualification achieved)
cons: Website lacks clear pricing and service tier information — no cost transparency for potential customers
No explicit FCRA compliance disclosures, terms of service, or guarantee limitations prominently displayed
Debt settlement and interest rate reduction claims (26% debt reduction, 47% lower rates) lack substantiation or disclaimers
Website content contains errors and incomplete sentences (e.g., 'How long does the process of credit repair take?' section cuts off mid-sentence)
Limited information on what happens if items cannot be successfully disputed or removed
best_for: Nevada residents with credit report errors or inaccurate negative items who want local, media-visible credit repair support
Consumers seeking to dispute specific negative items (collections, late payments, foreclosures) before mortgage or auto loan applications
Individuals who prefer transparent progress tracking and want to monitor disputes in real-time through a customer portal
People familiar with Harry Jacobs from his radio show or TV appearances who trust his media-established expertise
diagnosis: CRN Nevada is best suited for Nevada residents seeking credit dispute services from a locally established, media-visible operator with founder expertise in credit law and FCRA compliance. The primary caveat is the lack of transparent pricing, explicit compliance disclosures, and unsubstantiated claims around debt settlement and interest rate reduction, which are outside core credit repair and may signal scope creep into regulated debt relief services without clear disclaimers.
services: Free credit report analysis across all three credit bureaus
Dispute initiation via electronic and USPS communication to credit bureaus
Credit bureau monitoring and enforcement of 30-day response requirements
Customer portal access for real-time tracking of disputes and results
Removal of late payments, foreclosures, bankruptcies, and collections from credit reports
Removal of charge-offs, tax liens, and credit errors
Debt settlement services (up to 26% debt reduction claimed)
Credit card interest rate reduction negotiation (47% lower rates claimed)
Expert witness and legal consultation on credit damages (founder service)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
